rationale: The cited paper is conceptually close to the citing paper’s core topic: both study imagery-related processes and their effects on evaluations (citing note: dynamic imagery/perceived movement influencing brand attitudes; cited note: imagined self-use influencing product evaluations and choice). Both also emphasize fit/congruence conditions (logo movement cues with brand positioning vs contextual cue applicability), so the citation could plausibly be a theory/mechanism source rather than incidental background. The raw match is only a DOI-listed reference (index 22) with no in-text snippet, but this pair is not safely droppable.
